an7.12:1.1: “Monks, with the abandoning & destruction of the seven obsessions, the holy life is fulfilled.
an7.12:1.3: The obsession of sensual passion, the obsession of resistance,
an7.12:1.7: the obsession of passion for becoming,
an7.12:1.9: With the abandoning & destruction of these seven obsessions, the holy life is fulfilled.
an7.12:2.1: “When, for a monk, the obsession of sensual passion has been abandoned, its root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising;
an7.12:2.2: when, for him, the obsession of resistance…
an7.12:2.6: the obsession of passion for becoming…
an7.12:2.7: the obsession of ignorance has been abandoned, its root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising:
an7.12:2.8: this is called a monk who has cut through craving, has turned away from the fetter, and—by rightly breaking through conceit—has put an end to suffering & stress.”
